Pyrus communis is a deciduous tree species in the family Rosaceae, commonly known as the European pear or common pear. The health benefits of Pyrus communis include its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can help protect against chronic diseases such as cardiovascular disease and certain types of cancer. The bioactive constituents of Pyrus communis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Pyrus communis include pear juice, pear tea, and pear extract, which are used to support digestive health, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system.
